bias_direction: LOW / understated. Reported 0.8276 pct, corrected 1.3514 pct (+0.5238 points, +63.3 pct relative). The omitted organ contained the single largest per-cell win in the grid (L0.up_proj kron, 0.755 decades over the free rule), so dropping it pulled the portfolio's advantage toward zero. Absolute error levels also shift: the 27-cell means (0.356423 portfolio / 0.361306 free rule) are lower than the 18-cell means because up_proj cells are easier than down_proj cells, so the 18-cell and 27-cell absolute numbers are NOT comparable - only the margins are.

bit_charge.note: no bit charge changed. up_proj arms are charged by the same SHB.expert_bits / G.kron_bits calls at the same rung as gate_proj, and the shipped ledger already billed up_proj (coded_expert_tensors = 18048 = 94 layers x 64 kept experts x 3 organs). The omission was in the MEASUREMENT grid, not in the accounting, so incumbent_everywhere_complete_bpw 0.948410026538 and portfolio_complete_bpw 0.948410180077 stand and remain <= 1.0.

gamma_weighting_legitimacy: gvq IS legitimate for up_proj: up_proj's organ input is the post_attention_layernorm OUTPUT, exactly as for gate_proj, so the surrogate input model x = gamma (*) z and the column weight diag(gamma) are the same structurally-correct zeroth-order model there. gvq is NOT legitimate for down_proj, whose input is the SwiGLU intermediate that gamma does not describe; down_proj keeps the isotropic metric and the plain vq arm, unchanged.
honesty_caveats: NOT CAPABILITY. Surrogate output space on a synthetic input model x = gamma (*) z; no forward ran. The sealed S1 94-layer forward COLLAPSES 12/12 (symKL 7.7-8.6 vs a gate of 0.10) and nothing measured here is evidence against that.

CIRCULARITY, same as the parent module: for up_proj the predictor (gamma^2 anisotropy) and the scoring metric (gamma-weighted Frobenius) share gamma, so gvq's win in that metric is partly definitional. The isotropic control is reported per cell and gvq LOSES it in 9/9 up_proj cells (e.g. L0 0.230828 -> 0.761149), exactly as on gate_proj.

ASYMMETRIC ADMISSION BAR, inherited and declared: Kronecker must win BOTH metric spaces, gvq only the gamma-weighted one. Under Kronecker's own rule gvq would be refused 9/9 on up_proj and the free structural rule would collapse to plain vq there too.

FIT/SCORE OVERLAP, inherited: codebooks are fit on the same 3-expert cluster they are scored on, identically for every arm. This is the incumbent protocol, not a disjoint split, and it is NOT claimed as one.
COVERAGE: 9 of 94 layers measured for up_proj, matching the parent sample. The other 85 layers are unmeasured; nothing here predicts them.

DEEP-LAYER gvq GAIN IS TINY on up_proj: 0.04 pct at L93, 0.3 pct at L90, 2-9 pct at L1-L2 - in a metric gvq is definitionally fitted to. This does not justify switching codec on 93 of 94 layers; scope it to L0-L2 as the adversary recommended.
The 27-cell absolute means are not comparable to the 18-cell absolute means (different cell mix); only the margins are.

MEASUREMENT ONLY. No file in tools/ was modified; RUNGS['up_proj'] = RUNGS['gate_proj'] was injected at runtime in a scratchpad driver, so the codec, the rates and the bit charge are byte-for-byte the shipped ones.

best_universal_codec is reported as rvq for continuity with the shipped key, but rvq is NOT at equal complete bits (+12288 bits per tensor, 15745552 vs 15733264) and is inadmissible in all 27 cells including every up_proj cell. The like-for-like universal baseline is vq (0.371466 over 27 cells), against which the corrected portfolio margin is 4.0496 pct.

self_check.rerun: python3.12 tools/condense/qwen_codec_portfolio.py --self-check  (parent module logic), and this report's numbers are reproduced by measuring layers 0,1,2,5,18,46,70,90,93 with RUNGS['up_proj']=RUNGS['gate_proj']

verdict: HOLE CLOSED, VERDICT DIRECTION SURVIVES, SHIPPED NUMBERS DO NOT. All 9 up_proj cells were measured at the identical rung and identical exact bit charge as gate_proj (up_proj shares gate_proj's rung: dim8/k1024/stages2, 15733264 complete bits per tensor). The portfolio-vs-free-rule margin rises from 0.8276 pct over 18 cells to 1.3514 pct over 27 cells: the omission biased the reported margin LOW (understated by 0.5238 points, a 63 pct relative understatement). The shipped exception list ['L0.gate_proj'] is WRONG: L0.up_proj also admits Kronecker on all four conditions (concentration 0.999497, output 0.014315 vs free-rule gvq 0.081316 - a 0.755-decade win) at 15729152 <= 15733264 complete bits. The correct list is ['L0.gate_proj','L0.up_proj']. The other 8 up_proj cells select gvq, which coincides with the free structural rule, so the selector still buys nothing outside layer 0 and the 2-bit mode field is still not worth shipping. NOT A CAPABILITY CLAIM: surrogate gamma-input output space, no forward ran; sealed S1 collapses 12/12 and nothing here touches that.

wrong_exception_entries.why_wrong: incomplete - L0.up_proj:kron is admitted on all four conditions and is missing. Correct list is ['L0.gate_proj:kron','L0.up_proj:kron'].
the sample is 9 layers x 3 organs = 27 cells; up_proj is 6016 of 18048 coded expert tensors and 0 of them were measured.
18-cell means over a grid missing a third of the organ inventory.
gvq is legal on gate AND up, so it exists in 18 of 27 cells, not 9.

wrong_exception_entries.corrected: assignment now measured for up_proj on the 9 sampled layers: L0=kron, all others=gvq
wrong_exception_entries.why_wrong: key claims gate AND up coverage but the assignment was derived from gate cells only; up_proj was never in the organ loop.

wrong_exception_entries.corrected: add 'up_proj' to RUNGS and to the organ loop in build()
wrong_exception_entries.why_wrong: the assert passed while the code path it describes raised KeyError - it tested the rule function, never the measurement. Verified: measure_cell(...,'up_proj',...) raises KeyError with the shipped RUNGS.
